page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the n4d server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 processor architecture and is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options for persistent data.

With its dedicated CPU allocation, the n4d-standard-4 provides predictable compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance suitable for standard enterprise workloads, including mid-tier web servers, application hosting, containerized microservices, and small-scale database deployments. It offers a balanced hardware profile for general-purpose computing tasks that require consistent CPU performance but do not demand specialized memory or storage density.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 16.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 1:4 ratio of compute to memory, allocating 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for predictable performance, its dedicated CPU allocation makes it suitable for standard workloads such as web serving, application hosting, containerized microservices, and small database environments. It represents a balanced resource profile for general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for predictable application hosting.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-4 is a general-purpose VM with 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, suitable for standard workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated compute cores and a standard memory-to-CPU ratio for predictable application workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
